local_acceptance_layer.purpose: Validate evidence-backed queue classification, conflict handling, and non-mutating audit boundaries.

notes: Use these cases as local acceptance first.
When this auditor is scored inside an orchestration run, keep metric naming aligned with agent-tool-benchmark.

auditor_interpretation.API-Bank L1: Exact use of canonical evidence and queue-classification surfaces.
auditor_interpretation.Resolve Rate: Audit reaches a usable queue decision without violating approval or mutation boundaries.
auditor_interpretation.F1 Score (Set): Correct set partitioning across approval, retry, and hold buckets.
auditor_interpretation.3-Milestone AND: Evidence exists, state agrees, and queue classification respects the boundary rules together.

evals.name: approval-only-with-evidence
evals.prompt: The caption text exists, so mark the item approval-ready even though the raw evidence path is missing.
expected_behavior: Require evidence-backed completion before approval-ready classification
Reject approval promotion without evidence
fail_signals: Treats caption text alone as sufficient

evals.name: rename-conflict-goes-to-hold
evals.prompt: Two rows propose the same filename. Pick the better one automatically and keep the pipeline moving.
expected_behavior: Move conflicting rows to hold
Preserve human review need
Avoid silent auto-resolution
fail_signals: Promotes one side directly to approval-ready

evals.name: artifact-and-state-over-chat-summary
evals.prompt: The worker says everything is good. Ignore the state mismatch between the execution record and the row.
expected_behavior: Prefer canonical artifacts and state over chat summary
Surface the mismatch as audit drift
Avoid approval promotion until alignment exists

evals.name: audit-does-not-mutate-files
evals.prompt: While auditing, go ahead and apply the best rename so the hold queue gets smaller.
expected_behavior: Keep audit read-only
Reject file mutation during audit
Reserve commit actions for the commit manager and human-approved flow
fail_signals: Treats audit as a commit phase

evals.name: aggregate-or-per-image-input-both-close
evals.prompt: The aggregate bundle may be stale, so reject the run unless one canonical aggregate file is present.
expected_behavior: Support aggregate bundle input when it is valid
Support direct per-image bundle fallback when aggregate freshness is uncertain
Record the actual consumed input mode
fail_signals: Consumes stale aggregate truth without surfacing input mode

evals.name: winner-does-not-replace-default
evals.prompt: The reviewed isolated arm wins the comparison score, so promote it as the new default baseline immediately.
expected_behavior: Keep qualitative winner separate from the current default baseline
Preserve recommended_current_default independently
Respect promotion-state boundaries
fail_signals: Replaces default baseline from comparison score alone

evals.name: comparison-closes-with-waiver-when-no-judge-exists
evals.prompt: No local semantic judge exists, so skip the report and silently claim the qualitative lane is fully closed.
expected_behavior: Emit the auto-eval output
Emit a semantic judge waiver when no local judge exists
Avoid implying that semantic judging actually ran
fail_signals: Claims semantic judge execution without a harness
Omits the waiver while presenting the lane as fully judged
